pathaka: Asamyukta | Pataka - Flag. Fingers extended, thumb bent. Denotes clouds, forest, sea, night.
tripathaka: Asamyukta | Tripataka - Three parts of flag. Ring finger bent. Represents crown, tree, flame.
ardhapathaka: Asamyukta | Ardhpataka - Half flag. Last two fingers bent. Symbolises a knife or banner.
katrimukha: Asamyukta | Kartarimukha - Scissors. Index & little finger extended. Means separation or opposition.
mushti: Asamyukta | Mushti - Fist. All fingers closed, thumb on ring finger. Means holding or strength.
ardhachandran: Asamyukta | Ardhachandra - Half moon. Thumb extended outward. Used to hold or grasp objects.
chandrakala: Asamyukta | Chandrakala - Moon digit. Little finger raised. Represents the moon or beauty.
suchi: Asamyukta | Soochi - Needle. Index finger extended. Denotes oneness, the sun, or pointing.
kapith: Asamyukta | Kapitth - Wood apple. Thumb touches middle & index fingers. Offering to gods.
katakamukha1: Asamyukta | Katakamukha - Bracelet. Curved fingers. Used to hold a garland or flower.
sikharam: Asamyukta | Shikhar - Peak. Fist with thumb raised. Represents a bow or lover.
mukulam: Asamyukta | Mukul - Bud. All fingertips together. Represents a lotus bud or eating.
hamsasyam: Asamyukta | Hansaasya - Swan face. Thumb, index & middle tips together. Represents a swan.
hamsapaksha: Asamyukta | Hamsapaksha - Swan wing. Four fingers spread, thumb bent. Represents a swan.
bramaram: Asamyukta | Bhramara - Bee. Index finger on thumb, others spread. Represents a bee.
chaturam: Asamyukta | Chatur - Clever. Ring finger bent on thumb. Represents a serpent or bee.
tamarachudam: Asamyukta | Tamrachuda - Rooster. Thumb on middle finger. Represents a rooster or peacock.
alapadmam: Asamyukta | Alapadma - Full bloom lotus. All fingers spread wide. Represents a bloomed lotus.
garuda: Sanyukta | Garuda - Eagle. Thumbs interlocked, hands spread. Represents the eagle of Vishnu.
anjali: Sanyukta | Anjali - Salutation. Both palms joined together. Represents greeting and prayer.
berunda: Sanyukta | Berunda - Two-headed bird. Both hands spread as wings. Represents a mythical bird.
chakra: Sanyukta | Chakra - Wheel. Both index fingers and thumbs form a circle. Represents Vishnu's discus.
shivalinga: Sanyukta | Shivalinga - Symbol of Shiva. Right fist placed on left palm. Represents the divine.
swastikam: Sanyukta | Swastika - Auspicious cross. Wrists crossed at the chest. Represents auspiciousness.
kilaka: Sanyukta | Kilaka - Bond. Little fingers interlinked. Represents friendship or affection.
